chunk_summary: This section discusses clinical responses to food insecurity post-disaster, emphasizing program linkages for feeding, WASH counseling, and malnutrition management under Poshan 2.0 and PM POSHAN guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of Anganwadi and PM POSHAN programs in addressing food insecurity post-disaster.
Learn how to integrate feeding support with WASH counseling to prevent diarrheal diseases.
Identify appropriate malnutrition treatment pathways for children with acute illness or wasting.
Recognize the importance of breastfeeding support and regulated formula use in emergencies.
key_facts: Link families to safety nets within one week of a positive food insecurity screen.
Children under 6 years and pregnant/lactating mothers should be linked to Anganwadi services under Poshan 2.0.
School-age children should be connected to school meals under PM POSHAN guidelines.
Post-disaster, breastfeeding support and avoidance of unregulated formula distribution are critical.
WASH counseling should include handwashing with soap, safe drinking water, and sanitation to prevent diarrheal diseases.
Standard diarrhea management includes ORS and zinc supplementation.
reference_quote: For children under 6 years and pregnant or lactating mothers, link to Anganwadi supplementary nutrition and monitoring under Poshan 2.0 operational structures, including growth monitoring and counseling. (Reference 1)
document_key_topics: Integration of feeding and WASH counseling
Breastfeeding and formula regulation in emergencies
